user.title: Hermes Agent
user.description: Enter the connection details for your Hermes Agent instance.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to Hermes Agent. Check host and port.

abort.already_configured: This Hermes Agent instance is already configured.

data.enable_continued_conversation: Keep Home Assistant listening for follow-ups
data.enable_session_reuse: Reuse Hermes server sessions across short voice turns
data.always_speak_fallback: Always speak replies through fallback TTS

data_description.auto_follow_up: Keeps the voice assistant listening in the same conversation when Hermes ends with a question, including short clarifications after it. Hermes is also prompted to keep follow-up questions short and as the final sentence.
data_description.hide_tool_traces: Removes internal tool or shell command traces from displayed and spoken responses. When enabled, Hermes is also prompted not to expose them.
data_description.enable_continued_conversation: Always continue the conversation after a turn so Home Assistant keeps listening, regardless of whether Hermes ended with a question.
data_description.enable_session_reuse: Carry the Hermes server-side session across short voice turns so context survives wake-word re-engagement (uses X-Hermes-Session-Id).
data_description.session_timeout_seconds: Idle timeout before a remembered voice session expires. 0 disables expiry.
data_description.expose_device_context: Append the voice device's id, area, and satellite metadata to the system prompt so Hermes knows where the request came from.
data_description.always_speak_fallback: Also speak replies through the fallback media player / TTS entity below, useful if the satellite device can't speak the response itself.
data_description.fallback_media_player: Media player entity_id used when always_speak_fallback is on (e.g. media_player.living_room_speaker).
data_description.fallback_tts_engine: TTS entity_id used when always_speak_fallback is on (e.g. tts.cloud or tts.piper). Leave empty to use the default.
